description_long: This lantern features a traditional Japanese-inspired design with a square base and a tall, slender post supporting a lantern housing. The housing is topped with a multi-tiered, pagoda-style roof and has small, square windows with orange-tinted panes on each side, suggesting an internal light source. The asset includes two variations: one with a straight post and one with a gracefully curved post, both anchored to a solid rectangular base. The surfaces are smooth and appear to be made of stone or concrete, with the window panes likely made of colored glass. The overall structure is robust and intended for outdoor use, such as in parks, gardens, or along walkways. Decorative finials adorn the roof peaks, adding a subtle ornamental touch.
description: A pair of Japanese-style stone lanterns with square bases, pagoda roofs, and orange glass windows, one with a straight post and one with a curved post.
description_view_0: Front view showing both lanterns side by side, highlighting the straight and curved post designs and the orange window panes.
description_view_1: Side view emphasizing the depth of the lantern roofs and the thickness of the posts, with the window grid visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the lanterns, the solid bases, and the symmetry of the roof structures.
description_view_3: Angled view focusing on the curved post lantern, showing the lantern housing and decorative roof details.
